notes: Imagify is a well-secured plugin. Despite 20 flagged taint flows, every single one is a false positive. The plugin has consistent nonce + capability checks on all AJAX/admin-post endpoints. The one nopriv AJAX handler (nopriv_imagify_rpc) is a bridge for XML-RPC async jobs and is protected by transient-based user validation + nonce verification. Background processing nopriv handlers are standard wp-background-processing library with nonce checks. One informational finding: SSL verification disabled on API calls.

informational_findings.title: SSL Verification Disabled on Imagify API Curl Calls
informational_findings.description: CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST and CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER are both set to false in curl_http_call(). This is used when uploading images to the Imagify API (app.imagify.io). The Authorization header containing the API key is sent over this unverified connection. A network-level MitM attacker could intercept API keys. However, this requires network proximity, is not exploitable remotely by an unauthenticated attacker, and the API endpoint is hardcoded (not user-controllable), limiting SSRF risk.

imagify-sqli-001.reason: Data flows from internal DB query results ($wpdb->get_col) through cast_col() and prepare_values_list() (which calls esc_sql()) into another DB query. No user input enters this chain. The function remove_empty_inactive_folders() is only called internally.

imagify-sqli-002.reason: The $folders variable in the query at line 203 of class-imagify-files-list-table.php is constructed from internal database IDs, not from user input. The Files List Table is an admin-only page requiring authentication.

imagify-xss-001.reason: The imagify_check_coupon callback at line 888 has nonce verification (imagify_check_nonce) and capability check (current_user_can 'manage'). The coupon value goes through sanitize_text_field() and the response is JSON (wp_send_json_success), not HTML output. XSS in JSON responses requires the consumer to unsafely render the data.

imagify-rce-001.reason: The imagify_dismiss_ad_callback has nonce check and capability check. The $notice value goes through sanitize_text_field() and is stored in user meta. array_filter() is not an RCE sink. No shell execution anywhere in this chain.

imagify-rce-002.reason: maybe_unserialize() on database meta values is standard WordPress pattern. The data originates from $wpdb->get_results (database), not user input. Object injection via unserialize requires attacker-controlled serialized data in the database, which requires prior SQL injection or DB access -- not available here.

imagify-obje-001.reason: maybe_unserialize() on transient values from wp_options table. Data comes from database via $wpdb->prepare(). No user input controls the serialized data. Standard WordPress transient retrieval pattern.

imagify-rce-003.reason: absint($limit) is not user input -- it is a function parameter. maybe_unserialize() is applied to database results. This is internal admin statistics computation in NextGen Gallery integration, not reachable by unauthenticated users.

imagify-rce-004.reason: The $domain value comes from stored S3 settings (admin-configured option), not from user request input. sanitize_text_field() and preg_replace() are applied. preg_replace() is not an RCE sink (the /e modifier is not used).

imagify-rce-005.reason: absint() applied to blog IDs from $wpdb->get_col with $wpdb->prepare(). array_map('absint') is a sanitizer, not a sink. No shell execution. Admin-only upgrader code.

imagify-rce-006.reason: Same as rce-005. array_filter() on absint-processed database results is not an RCE vector.

imagify-xss-002.reason: ActionScheduler bundled dependency. absint() on $num_pastdue_actions, esc_url() on URL, wp_kses() on HTML output. Properly escaped. Admin-only notice.

imagify-rce-007.reason: ActionScheduler bundled dependency. $_GET['ID'] is processed through array_map('absint') which converts all values to non-negative integers. Nonce is verified. Not RCE.

imagify-xss-003.reason: ActionScheduler bundled dependency. esc_attr(), esc_url(), esc_html(), and absint() are all applied before output in the sprintf. Properly escaped.

imagify-rce-008.reason: ActionScheduler bundled dependency. array_map with a class method on prepared query results. Not RCE.

imagify-sqli-003.reason: ActionScheduler bundled dependency. $action_ids from $wpdb->prepare() query, then processed through array_map('absint'). The resulting integer-only string is safe for SQL IN clause.

imagify-rce-009.reason: get_option() returns admin-configured data. preg_replace with character whitelist [a-z0-9_-] is sanitization, not a sink. No shell execution.

imagify-rce-010.reason: Database result IDs processed through array_map('absint') and array_unique(). Not user input. Not RCE. Admin-only stats function.

imagify-sqli-004.reason: The $limit_ids variable in the SQL query is built from absint-processed post IDs retrieved from a prior database query. No user input flows into $limit_ids. Internal stats function.

imagify-rce-011.reason: maybe_unserialize() on database query results (post meta _imagify_data). Data is not user-controlled. Standard WordPress meta retrieval in admin stats context.

imagify-obje-002.reason: maybe_unserialize() on option values from wp_options. Same as obje-001 -- standard WordPress option retrieval pattern with no user-controlled serialized input.

imagify-rce-012.reason: Deprecated class. Database result IDs processed through absint and array_filter. Not user input, not RCE.

imagify-xss-004.reason: Deprecated class. wp_send_json_success() outputs JSON, not HTML. Data comes from database (filenames, optimization data). No direct HTML rendering.

imagify-xss-005.reason: The get_bulk_stats AJAX handler at line 569 of Bulk.php checks nonce (imagify-bulk-optimize) first, then filters by context with capability check. The filter_input from GET is authenticated. JSON response, not HTML.

imagify-rce-013.reason: Same pattern as rce-012 but in active Bulk/WP.php class. Database IDs through absint. Not user input, not RCE.

imagify-rce-014.reason: Same as rce-013 but for NextGen size optimization query. Database IDs through absint. Not user input, not RCE.
